text:
There once was a boy named Oliver who loved collecting stamps. Every spare moment, he turned his hobby into work by delivering mail for the local post office. Despite delivering mail for miles each day, Oliver never missed a day.

One particular day, Oliver received a peculiar letter. It was addressed to him with no return address, just a simple stamp on the front. Instead of money, there was a cryptic message written in an old language Oliver couldn't decipher. Intrigued, he brought it to the local librarian.

The librarian studied it and smiled. "That looks like an ancient code," she said. "You're a detective. Try decoding it."

Oliver spent days puzzling over the symbols. Finally, after consulting ancient texts and puzzling them out, he decoded it:

"In a realm where mountains turn to sand, the sun hides its light."

He thought about mountains turning to sand and realized the letter mentioned something about the desert. Digging his shovel into the yard, Oliver
